items.name: 4x UltraSharp Upscaler
items.author: Kim2091
items.description:
High-quality 4x upscaler. Excellent for photo-realistic upscaling.
One of the most popular upscalers in the AI image generation community.

items.name: BiRefNet DIS (Dichotomous Image Segmentation)
items.author: ViperYX
items.description:
High-quality background removal / image segmentation model.
BiRefNet (Bilateral Reference Network) trained on DIS5K dataset for
dichotomous image segmentation — excels at precise foreground/background separation.
Used in ComfyUI via ComfyUI_BiRefNet_ll custom node.

items.name: BLIP-2 OPT 2.7B
items.author: Salesforce
items.description:
BLIP-2 with OPT-2.7B language model from Salesforce.
General-purpose image captioning model.
Larger download (~6 GB) but produces natural language captions.

items.name: BSRGANx2 Upscaler
items.author: cszn
items.description:
2x upscaler based on BSRGAN (Blind Super-Resolution GAN).
Better than bicubic for real-world degraded/noisy/compressed images.
Handles JPEG artifacts, noise, and blur well — good for product photos
that have been through lossy compression.

items.name: Chroma
items.author: lodestones
items.description:
Open-source 8.9B text-to-image model based on Flux.1-schnell.
Apache 2.0 licensed, uncensored. Supports negative prompts.
Uses T5-XXL only (no CLIP text encoder needed).
Pruned from 12B to 8.9B by replacing modulation layers.
Community-driven with active LoRA ecosystem.

variants.note: Quantized to fp8 mixed precision. Good balance of quality and VRAM.

requires.reason: Chroma uses the same VAE as Flux (ae.safetensors)
T5-XXL for prompt processing (no CLIP needed)

items.name: CLIP-L Text Encoder
items.author: comfyanonymous
items.description:
CLIP-L (Large) text encoder. Used as secondary text encoder by FLUX models
alongside T5-XXL. Small and lightweight — 246 MB.

items.name: CLIP ViT-L/14
items.author: OpenAI
items.description:
OpenAI CLIP ViT-L/14 vision-language model. Used for aesthetic scoring,
image similarity comparison, and zero-shot classification. Foundation
model for the LAION aesthetic predictor.

items.name: CodeFormer
items.author: Shangchen Zhou
items.description:
Face restoration model using codebook lookup transformers.
Auto-detects faces and restores artifacts, blur, and damage.
No mask needed. Adjustable fidelity parameter (0=quality, 1=faithful).

items.name: Florence-2 Base
items.author: Microsoft
items.description:
Florence-2 Base — smaller variant of Microsoft's vision-language model.
Same capabilities as Florence-2 Large but with fewer parameters (~0.8 GB).
Faster inference, slightly lower caption quality.

items.name: Florence-2 Large
items.author: Microsoft
items.description:
Florence-2 Large is a foundation vision-language model from Microsoft.
Supports captioning, object detection, grounding, and OCR tasks.
Used by modl for auto-captioning datasets with detailed descriptions.
~1.5 GB download, runs on fp16 CUDA.

items.name: FLUX.1 Canny Dev
items.author: black-forest-labs
items.description:
FLUX.1 Canny Dev — canny edge conditioned generation model on FLUX architecture.
Structural control via canny edge maps. Gated model.

requires.reason: CLIP-L text encoder for FLUX
T5-XXL text encoder for FLUX
FLUX VAE

items.name: FLUX.1 Depth ControlNet
items.author: InstantX
items.description:
Depth-conditioned ControlNet for FLUX.1 Dev. Allows generating images
that follow the depth structure of an input image.

items.name: FLUX.1 Dev
items.author: black-forest-labs
items.description:
High-quality text-to-image model from Black Forest Labs.
Best results with 20-30 steps, CFG 3.5-4.0, euler sampler.
Non-commercial license. Requires accepting terms on HuggingFace.

variants.note: Quantized to fp8. Slight quality reduction vs fp16.

requires.reason: Flux models require the Flux-specific VAE (ae.safetensors)
T5-XXL for prompt processing
CLIP-L for secondary text encoding
Required for --style-ref

items.name: FLUX.1 Dev ControlNet Union Pro 2.0
items.author: Shakker-Labs
items.description:
Union ControlNet for FLUX.1 Dev by Shakker-Labs. Supports multiple control
conditions in a single model: canny, depth, pose, gray, softedge.
Pro 2.0 version with improved quality and stability.

requires.reason: Requires FLUX.1 Dev as the base diffusion model

items.name: Face/Portrait LoRA (Flux)
items.author: modl
items.description:
Training recipe for face/portrait LoRAs on Flux models.
Optimized for learning a specific person's likeness from 10-20 photos.
Produces a LoRA that generates consistent portraits with a trigger word.
Works with both flux-schnell (fast) and flux-dev (quality).

recipe.description:
Best for: headshots, portraits, consistent character identity.
Dataset: 10-20 high-quality face photos, varied angles/lighting.
Result: trigger word activates the learned face in any prompt.
tips: Use 10-20 photos with varied angles, expressions, and lighting
Avoid sunglasses, heavy makeup, or occlusions in training images
Caption with Florence-2 for best results — it describes face features well
If likeness is weak, increase steps to 2000 or rank to 32
If overfitting (same pose every time), reduce steps to 1000
Weight 0.7-0.9 is the sweet spot — too high causes artifacts

items.name: FLUX.1 Fill Dev
items.author: black-forest-labs
items.description:
FLUX.1 Fill Dev — inpainting and outpainting model based on FLUX architecture.
Supports masked image fill with text-guided generation. Gated model.

requires.reason: CLIP-L text encoder for FLUX
T5-XXL text encoder for FLUX
FLUX VAE

items.name: FLUX.1 Fill Dev OneReward
items.author: bytedance-research
items.description:
FLUX.1 Fill Dev fine-tuned with OneReward RLHF methodology from ByteDance Research.
Uses Qwen2.5-VL as a generative reward model for multi-task reinforcement learning.
Outperforms closed-source FLUX.1 Fill Pro on inpainting and outpainting tasks.
Drop-in replacement for FLUX.1 Fill Dev — same architecture, better quality.

variants.note: FP8 quantized. Recommended — best VRAM/quality tradeoff.

requires.reason: CLIP-L text encoder for FLUX
T5-XXL text encoder for FLUX
FLUX VAE

items.name: FLUX.1 Kontext Dev
items.author: black-forest-labs / unsloth
items.description:
FLUX.1 Kontext Dev — 12B parameter image editing model by Black Forest Labs.
Edit existing images via text instructions with robust consistency across
successive edits. Supports character/style/object reference without finetuning.
Trained with guidance distillation for efficient inference.
GGUF variants from unsloth, usable with ComfyUI-GGUF custom node.
Non-commercial license.

variants.note: Full precision bf16 GGUF. Best quality.
GGUF Q8_0 — highest quality quantization.
GGUF Q6_K — great quality/size balance.
GGUF Q5_K_M — recommended for 12GB GPUs.
GGUF Q4_K_M — good for 8GB+ GPUs.
GGUF Q3_K_M — budget option for lower VRAM.
GGUF Q2_K — smallest variant. Noticeable quality loss.

requires.reason: CLIP-L text encoder for FLUX
T5-XXL text encoder for FLUX
FLUX VAE for image decoding

items.name: Product Photography LoRA (Flux)
items.author: modl
items.description:
Training recipe for product photography LoRAs on Flux models.
Learn a specific product's appearance from 10-15 product photos.
Generate the product in new scenes, angles, and contexts.
Ideal for e-commerce, marketing, and social media content.

recipe.description:
Best for: product shots, e-commerce images, marketing visuals.
Dataset: 10-15 clean product photos on white/neutral backgrounds.
Result: trigger word places the exact product in any scene.
tips: Use flux-dev (not schnell) for product fidelity — details matter
Clean backgrounds in training images help the model isolate the product
Include multiple angles: front, side, 3/4, top-down
Florence-2 captions work well for product features
Higher weight (0.8-0.9) preserves product accuracy
Pair with a scene description for lifestyle shots

items.name: FLUX.1 Redux Dev
items.author: black-forest-labs
items.description:
FLUX.1 Redux Dev — image variation adapter for FLUX. Takes image input and
generates variations, similar to IP-Adapter functionality. Gated model.

items.name: FLUX.1 Schnell
items.author: black-forest-labs
items.description:
Fast text-to-image model from Black Forest Labs.
Optimized for speed — best with 1-4 steps, CFG 0 (distilled model).
Apache 2.0 license — free for commercial use.

variants.note: Quantized to fp8. Slight quality reduction vs fp16.

requires.reason: Flux models require the Flux-specific VAE (ae.safetensors)
T5-XXL for prompt processing
CLIP-L for secondary text encoding
Required for --style-ref

items.name: Style Transfer LoRA (Flux)
items.author: modl
items.description:
Training recipe for style transfer LoRAs on Flux models.
Learn an artistic style (illustration, anime, watercolor, etc.) from
15-30 reference images. The LoRA applies the learned aesthetic to any subject.

recipe.description:
Best for: artistic styles, illustration aesthetics, rendering techniques.
Dataset: 15-30 images in the target style, diverse subjects.
Result: trigger word applies the style to any prompt.
tips: More images = better style coverage. 20-30 is ideal
Include diverse subjects (people, landscapes, objects) all in the target style
Higher rank (32) captures style nuance better than faces
Lower learning rate (5e-5) prevents overfitting to specific images
Weight 0.5-0.8 blends naturally — 1.0 can be too strong
Combine with a face LoRA for styled portraits

items.name: FLUX VAE (ae.safetensors)
items.author: black-forest-labs
items.description:
The VAE used by all FLUX models. Required for FLUX.1 Dev and Schnell.
Same file (ae.safetensors) is bundled in both the Dev and Schnell repos.

items.name: FLUX.2 Dev
items.author: black-forest-labs / Comfy-Org / unsloth
items.description:
FLUX.2 Dev — next-generation image model from Black Forest Labs.
Up to 4MP photorealistic output with improved lighting, skin, fabric, and hand detail.
Multi-reference consistency (up to 10 images), improved editing precision,
better visual understanding, and professional-class text rendering.
Uses Mistral 3 Small text encoder and FLUX.2 VAE.
Open-source (non-commercial license).

variants.note: fp8 mixed precision quantization from Comfy-Org.
Full precision bf16 GGUF. Best quality, requires high-end GPU.
GGUF Q8_0 — highest quality quantization.
GGUF Q6_K — very good quality/size balance.
GGUF Q5_K_M — recommended for 24GB+ GPUs.
GGUF Q4_K_M — good for 24GB GPUs.
GGUF Q3_K_M — for 16GB GPUs. Some quality loss.
GGUF Q2_K — smallest, noticeable quality loss.

requires.reason: FLUX.2 VAE for decoding latents to images
Mistral 3 Small text encoder for prompt processing

items.name: FLUX.2 Klein 4B
items.author: black-forest-labs / Comfy-Org / unsloth
items.description:
FLUX.2 Klein 4B — the fastest model in the FLUX family.
Unifies text-to-image and image editing in one compact architecture.
Two variants: Base (undistilled, best for fine-tuning) and Distilled (4-step, ~1.2s on 5090).
Only 8.4 GB VRAM for distilled variant. Supports style transforms, semantic edits,
object replacement/removal, multi-reference composition, and iterative edits.

variants.note: 4-step distilled. ~1.2s on RTX 5090. Best for speed.
Undistilled base. Better for fine-tuning and maximum flexibility.
Full precision bf16 safetensors. Best quality, MPS-compatible.
Full precision bf16 in GGUF container.
GGUF Q8_0 — highest quality quantization.
GGUF Q6_K — very good quality/size balance.
GGUF Q5_K_M — recommended for 8GB GPUs.
GGUF Q4_K_M — good for 6GB GPUs.
GGUF Q3_K_M — for 6GB GPUs. Some quality loss.
GGUF Q2_K — smallest, noticeable quality loss.

requires.reason: FLUX.2 VAE for decoding latents to images
Qwen 3 4B text encoder for prompt processing

items.name: FLUX.2 Klein 9B
items.author: black-forest-labs / unsloth
items.description:
FLUX.2 Klein 9B — mid-size model in the FLUX.2 Klein family.
Successor to FLUX.1 with improved image quality and editing capabilities.
Supports text-to-image and image editing workflows.
Uses FLUX.2 VAE and Qwen 3 8B text encoder.
Available as BFL fp8 safetensors (distilled + base) and GGUF quantizations.
Requires ComfyUI-GGUF custom node for GGUF variants.

variants.note: 4-step distilled fp8 from BFL. Fast inference.
Undistilled base fp8 from BFL. Better for fine-tuning.
Full precision bf16 GGUF. Best quality.
Full precision fp16 GGUF.
GGUF Q8_0 — highest quality quantization.
GGUF Q6_K — very good quality/size balance.
GGUF Q5_K_M — recommended for 12GB+ GPUs.
GGUF Q5_K_S — smaller Q5 variant.
GGUF Q4_K_M — good for 8GB GPUs.
GGUF Q4_K_S — smaller Q4 variant.
GGUF Q4_1.
GGUF Q4_0.
GGUF Q3_K_M — for 8GB GPUs. Some quality loss.
GGUF Q3_K_S — smaller Q3 variant.
GGUF Q2_K — worst quality but smallest. Noticeable degradation.

requires.reason: FLUX.2 VAE for decoding latents to images
Qwen 3 8B text encoder for prompt processing

items.name: FLUX.2 Mistral 3 Small Text Encoder
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
Mistral 3 Small text encoder (bf16) for FLUX.2 Dev.
Required for FLUX.2 Dev text-to-image generation.

items.name: FLUX.2 Qwen 3 4B Text Encoder
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
Qwen 3 4B text encoder for FLUX.2 Klein 4B models.
Required for both base and distilled 4B variants.

items.name: FLUX.2 Qwen 3 8B Text Encoder
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
Qwen 3 8B text encoder (bf16) for FLUX.2 Klein 9B models.
Required for both base and distilled 9B variants.

items.name: FLUX.2 VAE
items.author: black-forest-labs / Comfy-Org
items.description:
VAE for all FLUX.2 models (Dev, Klein 4B, Klein 9B).
Different from FLUX.1 VAE — do not mix them.
Shared across all FLUX.2 model variants.

items.name: InsightFace Buffalo-L
items.author: InsightFace
items.description:
Face analysis model pack including SCRFD face detector, ArcFace
recognizer, and 2D/3D landmark predictors. Used for face detection,
identity matching, and face quality assessment in generated images.

items.name: IP-Adapter FaceID (SDXL)
items.author: h94
items.description:
IP-Adapter with InsightFace face ID embedding for SDXL.
Face-consistent generation from reference photos. Requires companion LoRA.

items.name: IP-Adapter SDXL (ViT-H)
items.author: h94
items.description:
IP-Adapter for SDXL using ViT-H image encoder. Enables image-prompted
generation by conditioning on reference images. Style transfer and composition.

items.name: LAION Aesthetic Predictor v2
items.author: LAION
items.description:
Tiny MLP that predicts aesthetic scores (1-10) from CLIP ViT-L/14
embeddings. ~3MB download. Requires clip-vit-large-patch14 for
feature extraction.

items.name: LCM LoRA (SD 1.5)
items.author: latent-consistency
items.description:
Latent Consistency Model LoRA for SD 1.5. Enables fast 2-8 step inference
via distillation. Drop-in acceleration for any SD 1.5 model.

items.name: LCM LoRA (SDXL)
items.author: latent-consistency
items.description:
Latent Consistency Model LoRA for SDXL. Enables fast 2-8 step inference
via distillation. Drop-in acceleration for any SDXL model.

items.name: LTX-2 19B
items.author: Lightricks
items.description:
LTX-2 19B parameter video generation model. Supports text-to-video,
image-to-video, video-to-video, and audio generation. Available in
full precision and multiple GGUF quantizations.

requires.reason: LTX-2 embeddings connector for prompt processing
LTX-2 video VAE for decoding

items.name: LTX-2 Embeddings Connector
items.author: Lightricks
items.description: Embeddings connector / text encoder for LTX-2 19B video model.

items.name: LTX-2 Video VAE
items.author: Lightricks
items.description: Video VAE for LTX-2 19B model. Handles video encoding/decoding.

items.name: LTX-Video 13B
items.author: Lightricks
items.description:
LTX-Video 13B parameter model (v0.9.8). Higher quality than the 2B version.
Includes distilled variant for faster inference. Supports text-to-video,
image-to-video, and multi-frame control.

requires.reason: T5-XXL for prompt processing

items.name: LTX-Video 2B
items.author: Lightricks
items.description:
Efficient 2B parameter video generation model by Lightricks. Fast inference,
supports text-to-video and image-to-video. Works best with long, descriptive prompts.

requires.reason: T5-XXL for prompt processing

items.name: Qwen Image 2512
items.author: Qwen / Comfy-Org / unsloth
items.description:
Qwen Image 2512 — 20B parameter text-to-image generation model by Qwen (Alibaba).
Updated 2512 revision with improved quality over the original Qwen-Image.
Based on Qwen 2.5 VL architecture. Generates high-quality images from text prompts.
This is the base text-to-image model, distinct from Qwen Image Edit which is
specialized for image editing tasks. Apache 2.0 license — free for commercial use.
Available as native safetensors (Comfy-Org) and GGUF quantizations (unsloth Dynamic 2.0).

variants.note: Full precision bf16 (2512). Best quality, needs A100 40GB+.
fp8 quantized (2512). Good balance of quality vs VRAM.
Full precision bf16 GGUF (2512).
GGUF Q8_0 (2512) — best quantized quality, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q6_K (2512) — great quality/size balance, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q5_K_M (2512) — recommended for 16GB+ GPUs, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q5_K_S (2512) — smaller Q5, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q4_K_M (2512) — good for 12GB GPUs, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q4_K_S (2512) — smaller Q4, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q3_K_M (2512) — budget option, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q3_K_S (2512) — smaller Q3, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q2_K (2512) — smallest, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.

requires.reason: Qwen Image VAE for decoding latents
Qwen 2.5 VL 7B text/vision encoder for prompt processing

items.name: Qwen Image 2512 Lightning LoRA
items.author: lightx2v
items.description:
Distillation LoRA for the standalone Qwen Image 2512 generation model.
Reduces inference from ~50 steps to 4-8 steps with minimal quality loss.
This is for the text-to-image model (Qwen/Qwen-Image-2512), NOT the edit model.
For the edit model, use qwen-image-edit-lightning instead.

variants.note: 4-step generation, bf16. Fastest variant.
4-step generation, fp32. Full precision.
8-step generation, bf16. Better quality than 4-step.
8-step generation, fp32. Full precision.

items.name: Qwen 2.5 VL 7B Text Encoder
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
Qwen 2.5 Vision-Language 7B text/vision encoder for Qwen Image Edit.
Handles prompt processing and image understanding for the Qwen Image editing pipeline.
Available in full bf16 (16.6 GB) and fp8 quantized (9.4 GB) variants.

variants.note: FP8 scaled quantization. Recommended — good quality at half the size.
Full bf16 precision. Best quality, needs more VRAM.

items.name: Qwen Image ControlNet Union
items.author: InstantX
items.description:
Unified ControlNet for Qwen-Image supporting 4 control types: canny,
soft edge, depth, and pose. 5 double blocks from the pretrained transformer.
Trained from scratch for 50K steps on 10M images at 1328x1328.
Recommended controlnet_conditioning_scale: 0.8-1.0 for all modes.
Use detailed prompts, especially when including text elements.

requires.reason: Requires Qwen Image as the base diffusion model

items.name: Qwen Image Edit
items.author: Comfy-Org / unsloth
items.description:
AI-powered image editing model based on Qwen 2.5 VL architecture.
Supports outpainting, format/ratio changes, AI scene generation, and in-place enhancement.
Best with 8 steps using Lightning LoRA, euler sampler, CFG 1.0.
Native safetensors are original 2509 (Comfy-Org). GGUF quantizations are the improved
2511 "Plus" version (unsloth Dynamic 2.0) with significantly better instruction following.

variants.note: Full precision bf16. Best quality, needs A100 40GB+ or similar.
High-quality fp8 quantization with sensitive layers in higher precision.
Mixed precision fp8 with comfy_quant layers. Sensitive layers kept in high precision.
Standard fp8 quantization. Good balance of quality vs VRAM.
NVIDIA fp4 quantization. Lower quality but fits on smaller GPUs.
2512 revision, full bf16 precision.
2512 revision, fp8 quantized.
2511 Plus revision, bf16. Improved instruction following over 2509.
2511 Plus revision, mixed fp8. Improved instruction following over 2509.
GGUF Q8_0 (2511) — best GGUF quality, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q6_K (2511) — good quality/size balance, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q5_K_M — recommended for 16GB+ GPUs.
GGUF Q5_K_S (2511) — smaller Q5, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q5_0 (2511), unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q5_1 (2511), unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q4_K_M (2511) — good for 12GB GPUs, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q4_K_S (2511) — smaller Q4, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q4_0 (2511), unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q4_1 (2511), unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q3_K_M (2511) — for 10GB+ GPUs, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q3_K_S (2511) — smaller Q3, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.
GGUF Q2_K (2511) — minimum quality, unsloth Dynamic 2.0.

requires.reason: Qwen Image Edit requires the Qwen-specific VAE
Qwen 2.5 VL 7B text/vision encoder for prompt processing

items.name: Qwen Image Edit Lightning LoRA
items.author: lightx2v
items.description:
Distillation LoRA for Qwen Image Edit that reduces inference from ~50 steps to 4-8 steps.
Multiple versions available: V1.0 and V2.0, in 4-step and 8-step variants.
Also includes Edit-specific variants for image editing (vs generation).
bf16 variants are half the size (~850 MB) with no quality loss on bf16/fp8 base models.

variants.note: Edit-specific, 8-step, V1.0. fp32 weights. Used by shopify-reframe-ai.
Edit-specific, 8-step, V1.0 in bf16. Half the size.
Edit-specific, 4-step, V1.0. Fastest but slightly lower quality than 8-step.
Edit-specific, 4-step, V1.0 in bf16.
Generation-focused, 8-step, V2.0. Latest version.
Generation-focused, 8-step, V2.0 in bf16.
Generation-focused, 4-step, V2.0. Fastest generation.
Generation-focused, 4-step, V2.0 in bf16.
Trained specifically for fp8 base model. 4-step, bf16.
Trained specifically for fp8 base model. 4-step, fp32.

items.name: Qwen Image VAE
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
VAE for the Qwen Image Edit pipeline. Single file, no variants.
Required by all Qwen Image Edit checkpoints.

items.name: Qwen2.5-VL 3B Instruct
items.author: Qwen
items.description:
Qwen2.5-VL 3B — vision-language model for image understanding, grounding, captioning, and OCR.
3.8B parameters, runs on 8GB consumer GPUs in fp16. Supports text-grounded object detection
(find objects by name), detailed captioning, automatic tagging, and text extraction.
Used by modl ground, modl describe, and modl tag primitives.

items.name: Qwen2.5-VL 7B Instruct
items.author: Qwen
items.description:
Qwen2.5-VL 7B — larger vision-language model for higher quality grounding,
captioning, and OCR. 8.3B parameters, needs ~16GB VRAM in fp16.
More accurate than the 3B variant, especially for complex scenes
and fine-grained object detection. Use --model qwen25-vl-7b for quality.

items.name: Qwen3-VL 2B Instruct
items.author: Qwen
items.description:
Qwen3-VL 2B — vision-language model for image understanding, grounding, captioning, and OCR.
2B parameters, runs on 4GB consumer GPUs in fp16. Supports text-grounded object detection
(find objects by name), detailed captioning, automatic tagging, and text extraction.
Default model for modl ground, modl describe, and modl vl-tag primitives.

items.name: Qwen3-VL 8B Instruct
items.author: Qwen
items.description:
Qwen3-VL 8B — larger vision-language model for higher quality grounding,
captioning, and OCR. 8B parameters, needs ~16GB VRAM in fp16.
More accurate than the 2B variant, especially for complex scenes
and fine-grained object detection. Use --model qwen3-vl-8b for quality.

items.name: RealESRGAN x4plus
items.author: xinntao
items.description:
General-purpose 4x upscaler from the Real-ESRGAN project.
Good balance of quality and speed. Works well for both
photos and AI-generated images.

items.name: Segment Anything (SAM) ViT-B
items.author: Meta
items.description:
Segment Anything Model (ViT-Base) from Meta. Given a point, box, or
automatic prompt, produces precise segmentation masks. Used for
targeted inpainting region selection.

items.name: Stable Diffusion 1.5
items.author: runwayml
items.description:
The original Stable Diffusion 1.5. Lightweight, fast, huge ecosystem
of LoRAs, embeddings, and ControlNets. 512x512 native resolution.

requires.reason: Recommended VAE for SD 1.5 (sharper outputs)

items.name: Stable Diffusion 2.1
items.author: stabilityai
items.description:
Stable Diffusion 2.1, fine-tuned from SD 2.0 with improved aesthetics.
Generates at 768x768 resolution. Uses OpenCLIP ViT-H/14 text encoder.

items.name: SD VAE ft-MSE
items.author: stabilityai
items.description:
Fine-tuned VAE for Stable Diffusion 1.5. Produces sharper, more
detailed outputs compared to the default VAE. MSE-optimized.

items.name: ControlNet v1.1 Canny (SD 1.5)
items.author: lllyasviel
items.description:
ControlNet v1.1 for SD 1.5 — Canny edge detection conditioned generation.
Provides structural control using canny edge maps. The most popular ControlNet.

items.name: ControlNet v1.1 Depth (SD 1.5)
items.author: lllyasviel
items.description:
ControlNet v1.1 for SD 1.5 — Depth map conditioned generation.
Uses MiDaS depth estimation maps for structural control.

items.name: ControlNet v1.1 OpenPose (SD 1.5)
items.author: lllyasviel
items.description:
ControlNet v1.1 for SD 1.5 — OpenPose body pose conditioned generation.
Uses human pose skeleton for structural control. Great for character posing.

items.name: Stable Diffusion XL Base 1.0
items.author: stabilityai
items.description:
Stable Diffusion XL base model. High resolution text-to-image generation
at 1024x1024. Works great with LoRAs and ControlNets.

requires.reason: Recommended VAE for SDXL (fixes fp16 NaN issues)
Required for --style-ref

items.name: ControlNet Canny (SDXL)
items.author: diffusers
items.description:
ControlNet trained on SDXL for Canny edge detection conditioning.
Single-file diffusers-format model for structural control in SDXL.

items.name: ControlNet Union (SDXL)
items.author: xinsir
items.description:
Unified ControlNet for SDXL by xinsir. Supports multiple control conditions
in a single model: canny, depth, pose, softedge, tile, scribble, hed,
mlsd, normal, segmentation, inpaint, and outpaint.

requires.reason: Requires SDXL as the base model

items.name: SDXL Refiner 1.0
items.author: stabilityai
items.description:
SDXL 1.0 Refiner — small-detail expert model. Used as a second pass
to add fine details to images generated by SDXL Base.

requires.reason: Recommended VAE for SDXL

items.name: SDXL Turbo
items.author: stabilityai
items.description:
SDXL Turbo — distilled from SDXL 1.0 using Adversarial Diffusion Distillation.
Generates images in 1-4 steps at 512x512. Great for real-time applications.

requires.reason: Recommended VAE for SDXL

items.name: SDXL VAE (fp16 NaN fix)
items.author: madebyollin
items.description:
Fixed SDXL VAE that works correctly in fp16 precision.
The original SDXL VAE produces NaN values in fp16 mode — this version
fixes that issue. Recommended for all SDXL workflows.

items.name: T5-XXL Text Encoder (fp16)
items.author: comfyanonymous
items.description:
T5-XXL text encoder in fp16 precision. Required by FLUX models
for prompt processing. Large model — 9.8 GB.
Use fp8 variant if you have less than 24 GB VRAM.

items.name: T5-XXL Text Encoder (fp8)
items.author: comfyanonymous
items.description:
T5-XXL text encoder quantized to fp8 precision. Uses half the VRAM
of the fp16 version with minimal quality impact.
Recommended for 12-16 GB VRAM setups using FLUX models.

items.name: UMT5-XXL Text Encoder
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
UMT5-XXL multilingual text encoder for Wan 2.1/2.2 video generation models.
Handles prompt encoding for all Wan model variants.

items.name: Wan 2.1 VACE 14B
items.author: Wan-AI / QuantStack
items.description:
Wan 2.1 VACE 14B — Video All-in-one Control & Edit model.
Unified 14B parameter model supporting inpainting, outpainting, subject-driven
generation, pose control, depth control, and more — all in a single model.
Apache 2.0 license — free for commercial use.
GGUF variants from QuantStack, usable with ComfyUI-GGUF custom node.

variants.note: Full precision bf16 GGUF. Best quality, needs A100 40GB+.
GGUF Q8_0 — highest quality quantization.
GGUF Q6_K — great quality/size balance.
GGUF Q5_K_M — recommended for 16GB GPUs.
GGUF Q4_K_M — good for 12GB GPUs.
GGUF Q3_K_S — budget option for lower VRAM.

requires.reason: UMT5-XXL text encoder for prompt processing
Wan 2.1 VAE for video decoding

items.name: Wan 2.1 VAE
items.author: Wan-AI
items.description:
VAE for Wan 2.1/2.2 14B video generation models. Compact 254 MB VAE
used by the T2V and I2V 14B models.

items.name: Wan 2.2 I2V 14B
items.author: Wan-AI
items.description:
Wan 2.2 14B image-to-video model (high-noise expert). Converts static images
into dynamic videos with smooth motion. Uses MoE architecture with separate
high-noise and low-noise experts.

requires.reason: Low-noise expert — required for Wan 2.2 MoE architecture
UMT5-XXL text encoder for prompt processing
Wan 2.1 VAE for video decoding

items.name: Wan 2.2 I2V Low Noise Expert 14B
items.author: Wan-AI
items.description:
Low-noise expert for Wan 2.2 14B image-to-video MoE architecture.
Used together with the high-noise expert for video generation.

items.name: Wan 2.2 T2V 14B
items.author: Wan-AI
items.description:
Wan 2.2 14B text-to-video model (high-noise expert). Uses MoE architecture
with separate high-noise and low-noise experts for cinematic-quality video.
Install this to get the full T2V 14B pipeline with all dependencies.

requires.reason: Low-noise expert — required for Wan 2.2 MoE architecture
UMT5-XXL text encoder for prompt processing
Wan 2.1 VAE for video decoding

items.name: Wan 2.2 T2V Low Noise Expert 14B
items.author: Wan-AI
items.description:
Low-noise expert for Wan 2.2 14B text-to-video MoE architecture.
Used together with the high-noise expert for video generation.

items.name: Wan 2.2 TI2V 5B
items.author: Wan-AI
items.description:
Wan 2.2 hybrid text-to-video and image-to-video 5B model. Fits on 8GB VRAM
with ComfyUI native offloading. Single model handles both T2V and I2V tasks.
Apache 2.0 license — free for commercial use.

requires.reason: UMT5-XXL text encoder for prompt processing
Wan 2.2 VAE for video decoding

items.name: Wan 2.2 VAE
items.author: Wan-AI
items.description:
VAE for Wan 2.2 video generation models. New high-compression VAE
used by the 5B TI2V model. 1.4 GB.

items.name: Z-Image
items.author: Tongyi-MAI / Comfy-Org
items.description:
Z-Image — undistilled 6B parameter foundation model from Alibaba Tongyi Lab.
Single-Stream DiT (S3-DiT) architecture. Supports full Classifier-Free Guidance
and negative prompting for precise control. High output diversity across seeds.
Better base for LoRA training and ControlNet than the turbo variant.
28-50 steps, CFG 3.0-5.0. Bilingual (English + Chinese) text rendering.
Z-Image-Turbo is a distilled version of this model for faster inference.

requires.reason: Qwen 3 4B text encoder for prompt processing
Z-Image VAE for decoding latents to images

items.name: Z-Image Qwen 3 4B Text Encoder
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
Qwen 3 4B text encoder used by Z-Image-Turbo.
Available in bf16 (full precision), fp8_mixed (reduced VRAM), and fp4_mixed (minimum VRAM).
Place in models/text_encoders/ and load with CLIPLoader node.

variants.note: Full precision bf16. Best quality text encoding.
Mixed fp8 quantization. Good quality with reduced VRAM.
Mixed fp4 quantization. Minimum VRAM, some quality reduction.

items.name: Z-Image-Turbo
items.author: Tongyi-MAI / Comfy-Org / jayn7
items.description:
Distilled 6B parameter text-to-image model from Alibaba Tongyi Lab.
Uses Scalable Single-Stream DiT (S3-DiT) architecture for maximum parameter efficiency.
Sub-second inference latency on H800 GPUs, fits within 16GB VRAM consumer devices.
Only 8 NFEs (steps) needed. Use euler sampler, guidance_scale 0.0.
Excels at photorealistic generation and accurate bilingual text rendering (English & Chinese).
Available as native safetensors (bf16/nvfp4) or GGUF quantizations.

variants.note: Full precision bf16. Best quality.
FP8 e4m3fn quantization. Half the VRAM of bf16, ideal for ControlNet on 24GB.
NVIDIA fp4 quantization. Lower VRAM usage with minimal quality loss.
GGUF Q8_0 — best GGUF quality. Requires ComfyUI-GGUF custom node.
GGUF Q6_K — good quality/size balance.
GGUF Q5_K_M — recommended for 8GB+ GPUs.
GGUF Q5_K_S — smaller Q5 variant.
GGUF Q4_K_M — good for 8GB GPUs.
GGUF Q4_K_S — smaller Q4 variant.
GGUF Q3_K_M — for 6GB+ GPUs. Noticeable quality reduction.
GGUF Q3_K_S — smaller Q3 variant.

requires.reason: Qwen 3 4B text encoder for prompt processing
Z-Image VAE for decoding latents to images

items.name: Z-Image-Turbo Fun ControlNet Union
items.author: alibaba-pai
items.description:
ControlNet union model for Z-Image-Turbo.
Supports Canny, HED, Depth, Pose, and MLSD control conditions.
Adjust control_context_scale (0.65–0.80) for best results.
Use with detailed prompts for better stability.
V2.0 with inpaint mode also available separately.

requires.reason: Requires Z-Image-Turbo as the base diffusion model

items.name: Z-Image-Turbo Fun ControlNet Union 2.1
items.author: alibaba-pai
items.description:
ControlNet union model v2.1 for Z-Image-Turbo with 8-step distillation.
Supports Canny, HED, Depth, Pose, MLSD, Scribble, and Gray control conditions.
Latest 2602 version adds gray control mode.
Adjust control_context_scale (0.65-1.00) for best results.
Use detailed prompts for stability.

Available in full (15 control layers, stronger control) and lite
(3 control layers, more natural results, lower VRAM) variants.

variants.note: Full 15-layer controlnet. Strongest control, best detail preservation. Needs ~7GB VRAM.
Lite 3-layer controlnet. Natural results, fits on 24GB with bf16 base model. ~2GB VRAM.

requires.reason: Requires Z-Image-Turbo as the base diffusion model

items.name: Z-Image-Turbo Distill Patch LoRA
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
Distillation patch LoRA for Z-Image-Turbo.
Used to enable the base Z-Image model to run with fewer steps (turbo mode).
Apply via LoraLoader node with strength 1.0.

items.name: Face/Portrait LoRA (Z-Image Turbo)
items.author: modl
items.description:
Training recipe for face/portrait LoRAs on Z-Image Turbo.
Similar to the Flux face recipe but optimized for Z-Image Turbo's
architecture — faster training, faster inference, lower VRAM.
Good option for 16GB GPU users.

recipe.description:
Best for: headshots and portraits on consumer GPUs (16GB).
Dataset: 10-20 high-quality face photos.
Result: fast inference with consistent likeness.
tips: Z-Image Turbo uses CFG 0.0 — don't add guidance
Only 8 steps needed — sub-second on a good GPU
Training is faster than Flux (~10 min on A100)
Quality is close to Flux Schnell but inference is faster
Good choice when you need volume over perfection

items.name: Z-Image VAE
items.author: Comfy-Org
items.description:
VAE for Z-Image-Turbo. Decodes latents to images.
Place in models/vae/ and load with VAELoader node.
